pg416-p1-s1:
wanted to talk about my many big problems; drinking
seemed a small one.
pg416-p1-s2:
And I knew that giving up “one
drink for one day” wouldn’t really do any good.
pg416-p1-s3: Finally, after seven months, I decided to try it.
pg416-p1-s4:
To this
day, I am amazed at how many of my problems—most
of which had nothing to do with drinking, I believed—
have become manageable or have simply disappeared
since I quit drinking.
pg416-p1:
wanted to talk about my many big problems; drinking
seemed a small one. And I knew that giving up “one
drink for one day” wouldn’t really do any good. Finally, after seven months, I decided to try it. To this
day, I am amazed at how many of my problems—most
of which had nothing to do with drinking, I believed—
have become manageable or have simply disappeared
since I quit drinking.
pg416-p2-s1:
I had already given up all the narcotics, most of the
pills, and some of the alcohol when I first came to A.A.
pg416-p2-s2:
By early July I had tapered off alcohol completely, and
I got off all pills in the ensuing few months.
pg416-p2-s3:
When the
compulsion to drink left, it was relatively easy to stay
off alcohol.
pg416-p2-s4:
But for some time, it was difficult to keep
from taking a pill when I had an appropriate symptom,
such as a cough, pain, anxiety, insomnia, a muscle
spasm, or an upset stomach.
pg416-p2-s6:
Today I feel I have used up my right to
chemical peace of mind.
pg416-p2:
I had already given up all the narcotics, most of the
pills, and some of the alcohol when I first came to A.A. By early July I had tapered off alcohol completely, and
I got off all pills in the ensuing few months. When the
compulsion to drink left, it was relatively easy to stay
off alcohol. But for some time, it was difficult to keep
from taking a pill when I had an appropriate symptom,
such as a cough, pain, anxiety, insomnia, a muscle
spasm, or an upset stomach. It has gotten progressively
easier. Today I feel I have used up my right to
chemical peace of mind.
pg416-p3-s1:
It helped me a great deal to become convinced that
alcoholism was a disease, not a moral issue; that I had
been drinking as a result of a compulsion, even though
I had not been aware of the compulsion at the time;
and that sobriety was not a matter of willpower.
pg416-p3-s2:
The
people of A.A. had something that looked much better
than what I had, but I was afraid to let go of what I
had in order to try something new; there was a certain
sense of security in the familiar.
pg416-p3:
It helped me a great deal to become convinced that
alcoholism was a disease, not a moral issue; that I had
been drinking as a result of a compulsion, even though
I had not been aware of the compulsion at the time;
and that sobriety was not a matter of willpower. The
people of A.A. had something that looked much better
than what I had, but I was afraid to let go of what I
had in order to try something new; there was a certain
sense of security in the familiar.
pg416-p4-s1:
At last, acceptance proved to be the key to my drinking
problem.
pg416-p4-s2:
After I had been around A.A. for seven
months, tapering off alcohol and pills, not finding the
program working very well, I was finally able to say,
“Okay, God.
